description: A designed Department of Labor card. The top half is a darkened, desaturated photograph of President Donald Trump outdoors (blurred White House colonnade/arches behind) wearing a navy blazer over a light blue shirt with an American-flag lapel pin, his right fist raised triumphantly in the air. Centered below the photo in large white serif type is the slogan 'Americans FIRST!' (with 'Americans' in italic and 'FIRST!' in bold). Beneath that sits a white rounded panel headed by the masthead 'THE NATIONAL LAW REVIEW' followed by black serif body text: 'Project Firewall is the most aggressive H-1B enforcement initiative in more than a decade:' followed by three bullet points: (1) 'It enables "secretary-certified investigations," giving the secretary of labor authority to personally initiate high-priority employer audits.' (2) 'The initiative expands interagency data sharing between DOL, Department of Homeland Security, and Department of State to create a more unified enforcement network.' (3) 'Early indications show targeted audits of employers with patterns of offsite placement, wage-level discrepancies, or unusually high H-1B ratios.' The technique pairs a triumphant authority image with a cited third-party legal publication to lend credibility to the administration's H-1B crackdown framed as protecting American workers. Target audience: American workers and the public supportive of restricting foreign-worker visas.
notable_text: Americans FIRST! | THE NATIONAL LAW REVIEW | Project Firewall is the most aggressive H-1B enforcement initiative in more than a decade
